name: R-CNN Architecture (Region-based Convolutional Neural Networks)
description: R-CNN (Region-based Convolutional Neural Network) is a multi-stage object detection pipeline that combines region proposals with deep learning. This visualization explores the original 2014 architecture which utilizes Selective Search for region extraction, a CNN for feature computation, and Linear SVMs for classification. While foundational, R-CNN is computationally expensive because it performs a forward pass for approximately 2,000 region proposals per image.
code:
graph TD
  Start[Input Image] --> SS[Selective Search Algorithm]
  SS --> RP[~2000 Region Proposals]

  subgraph Processing[Feature Extraction Pipeline]
    RP --> Warp[Warping / Resizing to 227x227]
    Warp --> CNN[Pre-trained CNN - AlexNet/VGG]
    CNN --> Feat[Feature Vector - 4096-dimensional]
  end

  subgraph Inference[Classification & Localization]
    Feat --> SVM[Class-Specific Linear SVMs]
    Feat --> BBox[Bounding Box Regressor]
  end

  SVM --> NMS[Non-Maximum Suppression]
  BBox --> NMS
  NMS --> Final[Final Detection Output]

  %% Styling
  classDef nodeStyle fill:#ffffff,stroke:#334155,stroke-width:2px,color:#000000
  class Start,SS,RP,Warp,CNN,Feat,SVM,BBox,NMS,Final nodeStyle

  style Processing fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style Inference fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
